jokes.text: Wow, your hair is really thinning
jokes.setup: Someone notices a colleague's baldness
jokes.punchline: Bluntly pointing it out with fake concern
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe humor - tactless observation that makes everyone uncomfortable. Economical but predictable.

jokes.text: Picture someone doing something heroic. Now, was he sitting or standing? Not counting FDR
jokes.setup: Dwight makes a point about standing being heroic
jokes.punchline: Has to immediately exclude the most famous disabled president
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific logic with perfect comedic timing. The FDR caveat transforms a silly argument into something genuinely funny through specificity.

jokes.text: Every second you sit there is an hour off your life
jokes.setup: Dwight promoting standing desks with health benefits
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated and impossible math delivered with authority
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight pseudo-science delivered with complete confidence. Good character integration but formula is familiar.

jokes.text: I feel like you're in a suicide cult
jokes.setup: Dwight looking at his sitting coworkers
jokes.punchline: Dramatically escalates office furniture to death cult
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation and economy. Dwight's tendency toward dramatic overstatement is well-executed here.

jokes.text: Hey, guys, let's just all admit it, okay? Dwight's better than us
jokes.setup: Jim addressing the room about Dwight's standing desk
jokes.punchline: False praise designed to trap Dwight into commitment
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim manipulation of Dwight. Sets up the prank payoff beautifully with false encouragement.

jokes.text: Taking a load off, huh? No. Putting a load on, more like
jokes.setup: Someone sees Dwight sitting and makes the obvious joke
jokes.punchline: Dwight's defensive wordplay to justify sitting
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay but shows Dwight's need to save face. Character-consistent but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Think fast. Ha ha... That's real creative
jokes.setup: Jim throws something at standing Dwight
jokes.punchline: Dwight's sarcastic response to basic prank
jokes.explanation: Generic prank setup. Dwight's criticism of Jim's effort is mildly amusing but predictable.

jokes.text: I could. I just don't feel like it, loser... Because you're sitting? Standing
jokes.setup: Jim keeps dropping things for standing Dwight to pick up
jokes.punchline: Dwight's stubborn refusal despite obvious inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the standing desk commitment. Dwight's stubbornness creates genuine conflict.

jokes.text: Andrew Baines Bernard... I love filling these out 'cause it's like Oh, I'm taking a test but I know the answers
jokes.setup: Andy filling out forms with his full name
jokes.punchline: His childlike joy at something incredibly mundane
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - finding joy in bureaucracy. Relatable but specific to his personality.

jokes.text: Under no circumstances can that be allowed to happen
jokes.setup: Robert says his wife can work there
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself privately to Andy
jokes.explanation: Strong setup for the episode's central conflict. Robert's duplicity is immediately clear and creates tension.

jokes.text: But if I were you, I wouldn't hire his wife
jokes.setup: Robert's obvious contradiction about hiring his wife
jokes.punchline: Jim's understated advice to camera
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim talking head - stating the obvious with perfect deadpan delivery. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: That's why my foundation, the Dream For A Wish Foundation, is gonna put them out of business
jokes.setup: Andy talking about competing with Make-A-Wish
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate business plan with knockoff name
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly inappropriate Andy moment. The knockoff foundation name and competitive attitude toward charity is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: We're 100% staffed up... Andy, let's see if there's a good fit first then we'll talk
jokes.setup: Andy tries to refuse hiring Susan
jokes.punchline: Robert immediately overrules him with polite threat
jokes.explanation: Good power dynamic comedy. Andy's attempt at honesty immediately crushed by Robert's authority.

jokes.text: Your path from obesity begins right here
jokes.setup: Dwight showing off his makeshift gym
jokes.punchline: Bluntly calling the customer obese as sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight tactlessness disguised as business acumen. Perfectly in character but somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: I used to say I wanted to live long enough to see a black president. I didn't realize how easy that would be. So now I want to live long enough to see a really, really gay president or a supermodel president
jokes.setup: Creed explaining his fitness motivation
jokes.punchline: Casually racist assumption followed by increasingly absurd presidential fantasies
jokes.explanation: Peak Creed absurdity with perfect character voice. The escalation from reasonable to bizarre is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Chairs wear out faster. It takes more freon to keep them cool. They flush the toilets more often. Plus, their massive BMs bust the rivets on my pipes
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why obese people are expensive
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous and gross justifications
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect Dwight pseudo-logic. Each reason gets more absurd and specific.

jokes.text: But a gym turns fat into cash
jokes.setup: Dwight's elaborate explanation of obesity costs
jokes.punchline: Reduces complex scheme to simple capitalist motto
jokes.explanation: Perfect distillation of Dwight's business philosophy. Excellent economy and character integration.

jokes.text: It's actually Ryan... Oh, Ryan. Yeah... Bitch
jokes.setup: Susan politely corrects Ryan's name
jokes.punchline: Ryan's inexplicable hostile response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan character moment - inexplicably rude for no reason. The misdirection works well.

jokes.text: I was watching a movie and Meryl Streep had two secretaries... I was watching a movie and a bunch of apes took over San Francisco
jokes.setup: Erin uses movie logic to justify two receptionists
jokes.punchline: Ryan's reductio ad absurdum with Planet of the Apes
jokes.explanation: Excellent logical comedy. Ryan's response perfectly deflates Erin's movie logic with absurd parallel.

jokes.text: Sales is really not so hard, okay It's paper. We have it, and they want it so bad they are willing to pay for it
jokes.setup: Explaining sales complexity to Susan
jokes.punchline: Reduces entire profession to absurdly simple transaction
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight over-simplification. Character-consistent but familiar formula.

jokes.text: It's the second-easiest job in the world... Being a mom
jokes.setup: Dwight claims sales is second-easiest job
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's completely wrong guess about what's first
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection joke. Phyllis's response is characteristically tone-deaf but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: What I wouldn't give for one of Phyllis's classic room-clearing farts right now
jokes.setup: Andy desperate to make Susan leave
jokes.punchline: Hoping for disgusting bodily function as solution
jokes.explanation: Good character desperation humor. The specificity of 'room-clearing' elevates basic bathroom humor.

jokes.text: This is not a gym. This is like a scene out of Saw V
jokes.setup: Darryl sees Dwight's makeshift gym equipment
jokes.punchline: Compares workout equipment to torture devices
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy reference. The Saw comparison is apt and economical.

jokes.text: Obviously, you know how a gym works. So you know I'm not gonna pay money for this
jokes.setup: Dwight's confusing pricing structure
jokes.punchline: Darryl's matter-of-fact rejection
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Dwight's absurdity. Darryl's practicality grounds the scene.

jokes.text: I am the CEO, and I am telling an employee of mine what to do. Let me beat around the bush for a second
jokes.setup: Robert asserting his authority
jokes.punchline: Says he'll 'beat around the bush' while being direct
jokes.explanation: Mild irony in Robert's contradictory phrasing. Character-appropriate but not exceptionally clever.

jokes.text: Say 'hello, grandma.'... Hi, gam-gam
jokes.setup: Robert coaching Andy on phone call
jokes.punchline: Andy's childish pronunciation of 'grandma'
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing character moment showing Andy's childlike tendencies under pressure.

jokes.text: You numbskull! You were given a very simple task
jokes.setup: Robert's fake phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Switches to angry berating mid-performance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Robert's manipulative nature. The performance aspect adds layers.

jokes.text: We promised we'd never say good-bye
jokes.setup: Robert asks why Andy didn't say goodbye
jokes.punchline: Andy's overly sentimental explanation
jokes.explanation: Sweet Andy character moment that explains his compliance. Good character integration.

jokes.text: We have a gym at home. It's called the bedroom. Who's spotting who?
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining why he doesn't need a gym
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about bedroom activities
jokes.explanation: Basic Kevin sex joke. Character-appropriate but predictable and cheap.

jokes.text: You gotta get the black people to do it to get the white people to do it. Then you gotta get the black people to stop doing it
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining business strategy
jokes.punchline: Casually racist marketing theory
jokes.explanation: Dark Dwight humor showing his problematic worldview. Character-consistent but uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Who walks around with their passport? Well, I do. Always ready for adventure. Have you had any? No
jokes.setup: Susan asks about passport, Oscar explains he carries his
jokes.punchline: Admits he's never actually used it for adventure
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment for Oscar. The contradiction between preparation and action is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: How can we do that? She's simply wonderful
jokes.setup: Andy asking everyone to be mean to Susan
jokes.punchline: Coworker's genuine appreciation undermining the plan
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing how Andy's plan is doomed. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I know exactly which stapler to give her
jokes.setup: Erin assigned to make Susan uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: References the jello stapler prank
jokes.explanation: Good callback for fans but relies heavily on external reference rather than current humor.

jokes.text: That's Gerald... Oh, wow. So cute... Is he in a ladle? Yeah, he's in a ladle
jokes.setup: Angela showing photos of her cats
jokes.punchline: Cat literally sitting in kitchen ladle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela character moment - obsession with cats reaches absurd level. Visual is genuinely funny.

jokes.text: Excuse me, waiter there's a dog in my soup... It's not that kind of ladle
jokes.setup: Susan makes a joke about the cat in the ladle
jokes.punchline: Angela's literal correction missing the humor
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Angela's literal-mindedness vs Susan's attempted humor.

jokes.text: Last time I had a job, I remember I hated the boss' wife. Of course, she was married to Robert
jokes.setup: Susan relating to disliking boss's wife
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was the boss's wife and it was Robert
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect setup and payoff. Adds character depth while being genuinely funny.

jokes.text: I should step out, get to the ATM before that homeless man sets up camp for the night
jokes.setup: Robert excusing himself from awkward situation
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions homeless person's routine at ATM
jokes.explanation: Classic Robert California non-sequitur revealing his detached worldview. Dark but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Okey-dokey, artichokey
jokes.setup: Andy responding to Robert's exit
jokes.punchline: Childish rhyming phrase showing his discomfort
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy nervous humor. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: My husband can be a very difficult man to read, can't he? Yes. The dude is an enigma
jokes.setup: Susan asking about Robert's communication style
jokes.punchline: Andy's agreement with casual modern slang
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's casual response to formal question. Mild humor in word choice.

jokes.text: Is that the same philosophy you apply to buffalo wings? I want you to bring that same buffalo wing passion to this gym
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining his slow gym approach
jokes.punchline: Dwight connecting it to Kevin's eating habits
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor playing off Kevin's known eating habits. Well-constructed parallel.

jokes.text: I'm gonna make you look like Lebron James... It's Lejon Brames
jokes.setup: Dwight promising to transform Kevin
jokes.punchline: Kevin's mispronunciation of LeBron James
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin malapropism. Simple but effective character humor with great quotability.

jokes.text: Robert storms in and he says 'In four seconds my wife's gonna be in here, I told her she could work here, Under no circumstances can that be allowed to happen.' You lying son of a bitch
jokes.setup: Andy recounting Robert's earlier contradiction
jokes.punchline: Robert's immediate hostile response to being exposed
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation and payoff to earlier setup. Robert's immediate aggression is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: He just rolled out and crawled out
jokes.setup: Looking for Jim who escaped the confrontation
jokes.punchline: Description of Jim's dramatic stealth exit
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy description. Jim's escape method is perfectly in character and efficiently described.

jokes.text: Creed, I was never here, all right? Okay. What about your friend?
jokes.setup: Jim hiding and asking Creed to cover for him
jokes.punchline: Creed's confusion about Jim's 'friend' (the camera)
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor with Creed acknowledging the documentary cameras. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: You wish that every exercise was strutting around the gym like the Fonz? Well, how do you think the Fonz got so cool? He stretched his pelvic bowl
jokes.setup: Dwight criticizing Kevin's exercise reluctance
jokes.punchline: Bizarre theory about Fonzie's coolness coming from stretching
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight logic connecting fitness to pop culture icon. Original and perfectly character-specific.

jokes.text: She literally makes me work harder. She makes me smarter. She makes me remember why I'm here. And between us she's on maternity leave right now and I would love to leave this room and see her face
jokes.setup: Jim explaining working with spouse
jokes.punchline: Genuine emotional revelation about missing Pam
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment balancing humor with genuine emotion. Perfect Jim sincerity breaking through the comedy.

jokes.text: You're nothing! You're so weak! You call yourself a man? This doesn't help me. I don't respond to that kind of strategy
jokes.setup: Dwight trying aggressive motivation on Kevin
jokes.punchline: Kevin's calm, therapy-speak response
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Dwight's aggression and Kevin's unexpected self-awareness. Character growth humor.

jokes.text: To look good for Val... Val Kilmer? I don't buy it that doesn't make any sense
jokes.setup: Kevin reveals his fitness motivation is 'Val'
jokes.punchline: Dwight's confusion about Val Kilmer vs Kevin's girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on name confusion. Dwight's literal interpretation is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I am gonna make you the buffest dude Val Kilmer has ever seen
jokes.setup: Dwight finally understanding Kevin's motivation
jokes.punchline: Still thinks it's about the actor rather than girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Perfect conclusion to the confusion. Dwight's commitment to the wrong interpretation is perfectly in character.
